pattern: Flooding is monotone over time, so binary search the latest day and test top-to-bottom connectivity through the remaining land.
hints: If you cannot cross on a given day you certainly cannot cross any later day, which makes the answer the boundary of a monotone predicate.
Fix a candidate day, flood every cell scheduled up to that day, and ask whether any land path links the top row to the bottom row.

Binary search on the day. For a midpoint, build the grid with the first mid floods applied, then run a BFS/DFS or union-find from the top row; if it reaches the bottom row the day is feasible, so search higher, otherwise search lower.

edge_inputs.description: 2x2 floods leave a path until day 2
2x2 floods both columns by day 2 but cross only until day 1
single column, any flood blocks immediately
single row, crossing is free until that row floods
first flood already cuts the only narrow bridge
tall thin grid floods a side first

stress.description: random flood permutation over a modest grid
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import math
    target = max(4, min(size, 600))
    row = rng.randrange(2, 21)
    col = max(2, (target + row - 1) // row)
    col = min(col, 60)
    coords = [[r, c] for r in range(1, row + 1) for c in range(1, col + 1)]
    rng.shuffle(coords)
    return [row, col, coords]
stress.note: cells is a full permutation of all grid coordinates
